content:
The card-hand interactions have pretty big problems with their implementation (at least on PC).

While there seems to be no issues with just taking a card and playing it, for some reason there is a bunch of issues/strange interactions occur when you try to put the card back in your hand. 

In the video I attached, I demonstrated quite a few examples of those issues:
   1) When a card reaches the bottom of the screen, it pops up into the "card preview" mode, which is very unintuitive, since by sliding the card down, most of the time you just want to place it back in hand. This "card preview" mode also automatically enables a switch to another card if your cursor shift to it too much.
   2) How low the card has to go to return to the hand (as well as how low it can go), is wildly different depending on where you clicked on that card when taking it. Throughout the video I demonstrate how I take the cards by clicking on different sections of the card (top, middle, bottom, corner) and each time it changes how low the card needs to go or even at what place (if you take a card by the right corner, then it want's to be placed more to the left to go back in hand)
   3) As mentioned in the previous point, cards don't want to go back into hand if they were shifted to left or right, even a little in some cases. You have to put them exactly in their place, which can be really hard because of the problem I stated in the previous point. So even if you lower the card, but missed it's spot, it will be played. It also seem to have more strange interactions with the cards that lay on the edges of your hand. Even though it wasn't in the video, I was able to drag a leftmost card to the left corner of my screen without it activating, but in over cases it wouldn't allow it and just activate (I think it also depend on things stated in the 2nd point).

Those things negatively impact the experience with the most basic mechanic in the card games - placing you card back in hand.

3.1) Later I discovered that if I drag the card that I took to the sides of my screen and then slide them down along that side to the corner, they become deactivated (i.e. ready to come back into hand). It seems to be because it doesn't trigger switch to "card preview" mode in those places.

As for solutions. While points 2 and 3 can be tricky to fix, depending on how they were implemented, the fastest and most impactful fix will be to disable the auto switch between "holding the card" mode and "preview the card" mode (point 1), by making "preview mode" disabled while the user "holds the card". Because those two mechanics are clearly clashing with each other, making points 2 and 3 way worse, since if "preview mode" was disabled you could place the card as low as you want (like on the sides of the screen), without it being "stuck on the edge" right before switching to "preview the card" mode.

Please fix this, as this is one of the core interactions in the card games. And it's frustrating to no end when in the difficult fight you took out a card while still thinking, changed your mind and then it got activated, essentially ruining the turn, or the whole fight. 

To be honest, when I just discovered this game and tutorial started, this issue is what I faced in the very first battle of the tutorial, and closed the game afterwards. It's just that scuffed, and makes the game feel unpolished and clunky. I returned only a few months later, when I heard that the story, music, art and the rest of the gameplay there quite great, and decided to give it another try, and fell in love with the game. I even spend some money when usually I play as a pure f2p. The game is that good.

I think this is a pretty glaring issue, because the gameplay is the very first thing you're experiencing in this game, and as such it can leave a very bad first impression on the players even before they get the chance to really know the game and it's strengths.

<@&1166257943742787615> please read it, I really think this is an important issue, that significantly affecting the game in a negative way, and have a fairly simple solution (or at least partial solution). Thank you.

原來 感謝

While I understand that it's not always feasible due to time and budget limitations, it would still be great to see more well thought out kit adjustments and reworks, rather than just value increases. For that reason, I would like to offer some ideas for the characters I'm especially passionate about

Ever since the start of arc 2, Pandia has been regarded as one of the weakest characters in the game, with the majority of playerbase only ever considering her for the niche utility of removing counter from enemies, completely ignoring the rest of her kit. 
To expand on the idea of Pandia being a damage dealer that excels at dealing with multihitting enemies, I have these suggestions: 

- Over-exaltation: 
Pandia's OE effect is one of the most underwhelming in the game, only providing one turn vulnerability application and a small amount of fixed counter. 
Whether as a replacement of the current effect or as an addition to it, it would be great if the OE would temporarily increase the number of damage instances of Pandia's command cards. Perhaps the number of bonus damage instances could depend on the enemy's intention, increasing further if the enemy is attacking multiple times, expanding on the idea introduced with the addition of her soulforge aptitude.
That would allow Pandia to make a better use of her rouse, which is currently pretty much never used by anyone.
It would also go well with the character's themes, since the enemy version of Pandia also increases the number of hits on her Whip of Madness attack whenever you strike her.

- Absolute Axiom:
The AA Pandia got wasn't very impressive, as the permanent counter she generates isn't very high, and 3% of a not very high number is a barely noticeable number.
To make Pandia more effective at her job, perhaps her Absolute Axiom effect could be changed to her temporarily stealing a small portion of enemy strength whenever she generates counter, giving her a little bit of defensive utility against multiattacks.

thread_title: Some passionate feedback on Pandia's 2.5.1 buffs

content:
Hi, I have written another post  here a while ago, asking about a buff for Wanda (my favorite character). I would like to start by thanking you guys for listening to the community and the efforts you have put into the game. 

I would also like to say that I appreciate the buffs Wanda received; however, I'm a bit worried that this doesn't tackle the real issues with her kit. Most of the buffs that I have seen in the DEV logs are numerical adjustments, and I'm pretty sure there's a reason for that. 

Those adjustments, for a lot of characters, are very welcome, but  in my opinion, one of the worst problems with Wanda is that her cards cost WAY TOO MUCH. I'm pretty sure her "Keeper of the Lost" command card is one of the only (if not THE only) high-cost command card in the game that doesn't get Retain+Prepared from any enlighten level, and that gets even worse when you consider that it is usually a reactive card.

Could you guys please consider making this change in the card? If it's not possible, I will try to understand, but I genuinely think it is needed for her.



Thank you again for listening.

I think this is a great idea. Wanda is kinda underwhelming for a limited unit; she still needs some fine-tuning to feel strong. also, about high-cost command cards without prepare+retain, there's another example: sanga's 4-cost. I always thought Sanga should gain prepare + retain on that card with one of her enlightens, because why not? It wouldn't even be broken, to be honest. And her comps are kinda niche, we need more love for stalling comps too.

不知道是我對機制不太熟悉？還是命運裁斷追擊的時候影響到什麼？或者超維空間有手牌上限限制？又或者右下的手牌上限顯示錯誤了？<:Helot_slack_off:1459092159465521184>
